note: Race metadata for the 2026 Austin City Council cycle. Each entry drives one race view page at /austin/<race_id>/ via race_template.html. Adding a district = add one entry here + run build_race.py --race <race_id>. Candidate stats are on a common 2026-cycle basis (contributions from 2025-01-01 forward) so candidates who have run before are compared on this race only, not lifetime totals.

races.title: Austin City Council District 1
races.intro: District 1 is an open seat. Natasha Harper-Madison, who has represented the district since 2019, is not seeking a third term. Austin's two-term limit permits a third term if a member gathers signatures from 5% of registered voters in the district; Harper-Madison declined to pursue that petition. Her term ends January 6, 2027.
races.context_note: Candidate filing runs July 20 - August 17, 2026. Everyone listed here has declared and appointed a campaign treasurer, but filing has not closed. Expect additions, and expect some listed candidates not to complete filing.

candidates.name: Amber Goodwin
candidates.bio: Assistant district attorney in the Travis County DA's office; founder of the Community Justice Action Fund and former chair of Austin's Gun Violence Prevention Task Force.

candidates.name: Misael Ramos
candidates.bio: Community organizer and affordable-housing development professional; president of the Rogers Washington-Holy Cross Neighborhood Association. Ran for this seat in 2022, finishing second.

candidates.name: Alexandria Anderson
candidates.bio: Small-business owner and certified personal trainer; retired Nike-sponsored sprinter and UT graduate; president of the MLK Neighborhood Association.

candidates.name: Steven Brown
candidates.bio: Born and raised in East Austin; clinical specialist for medical-technology firm Medtronic. Formerly a co-chair of the Save Austin Now PAC.

candidates.name: Portia Riggins
candidates.bio: Third-generation East Austinite raised near 12th & Airport; background in accounting, finance and administration; finishing an associate's degree at Austin Community College.

candidates.name: Michael Nahas
candidates.bio: Computer science and economics background; housing-supply advocate.
candidates.placeholder_note: Filed to run - no campaign finance data on file yet

omitted.name: Kyra Rogers
omitted.reason: Reported as a candidate by a single outlet we could not independently verify. Will be added once confirmed against the City Clerk's treasurer-appointment list.
